pi_items.source: SOC code 49-9071.00 verified via O*NET, ONET Code Connector at https://www.onetcodeconnector.org/ccreport/49-9071.00, and BLS databases. Job titles include Building Mechanic, Facilities Technician, Maintenance Specialist.

BLS Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) system. O*NET Code Connector at https://www.onetcodeconnector.org/ccreport/49-9071.00 confirms this code corresponds to Maintenance and Repair Workers, General.

NAICS Sector 53 (Real Estate and Rental and Leasing) confirmed via https://www.naics.com/naics-code-description/?v=2017&code=53. Task description states 'Facilities Planner at a commercial real estate firm that manages multiple office buildings' (task.json line 5).

Task description (task.json line 5): 'create a quarterly HVAC preventive maintenance schedule covering all three office buildings in the system' and README.md line 16 'Create three new PM activity cards — one per building'. Multi-building work order task also confirms 'three buildings in the system'.
PM requirements file created in setup_task.sh line 122: 'Filter replacement - Replace all HVAC air filters (MERV-13 rated)'
PM requirements file created in setup_task.sh line 123: 'Coil cleaning - Clean evaporator and condenser coils'
PM requirements file created in setup_task.sh line 124: 'Refrigerant level check - Verify refrigerant charge levels'. Metadata is shortened form of full requirement.
PM requirements file created in setup_task.sh line 125: 'Thermostat calibration - Calibrate all zone thermostats within ±1°F'
PM requirements file created in setup_task.sh line 126: 'Condensate drain inspection - Clear and inspect all condensate drain lines'. Metadata is shortened form of full requirement.

Task description (task.json line 5) specifies '90 days or quarterly'. Verified via HVAC maintenance standards: quarterly (90-day) frequency is standard for commercial HVAC systems, especially for filter replacement and coil cleaning (https://oxmaint.com/industries/hvac/commercial-hvac-preventive-maintenance-schedule-by-frequency).
Code analysis: cmdbuild_api.py lines 200-214 and setup_task.sh lines 26 define find_pm_class() function that searches for patterns like 'PreventiveMaint', 'PreventiveActivity', 'Preventive', etc.
docker-compose.yml (config/docker-compose.yml line 33) environment variable CMDBUILD_DUMP=demo.dump.xz. Buildings are loaded from demo database; PM data is created by agent during task execution.

privileged_info_summary: The preventive maintenance schedule setup task is for a Facilities Planner (SOC 49-9071.00, Maintenance and Repair Workers, General) at a commercial real estate firm managing three office buildings. The task requires creating three new preventive maintenance activity cards (one per building) with code pattern PM-HVAC-Q-{BuildingCode}, each with all five required maintenance tasks: filter replacement, coil cleaning, refrigerant level check, thermostat calibration, and condensate drain inspection. Frequency is set to 90 days (quarterly), which is standard for commercial HVAC maintenance. Priority must be set to medium/normal. The PM requirements are documented in /home/ga/Desktop/pm_requirements.txt created during setup. The task operates on OpenMaint's synthetic demo database containing three pre-configured office buildings from demo.dump.xz.
